verses.text: The LORD tells Moses: turn back and camp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea. Pharaoh will say, they are tangled — the wilderness has shut them in. I will harden his heart; he will pursue. I will be honored upon Pharaoh, and Egypt shall know I am the LORD. Guidance: God sometimes directs His people into a trap to spring a bigger one on their pursuers. The cul-de-sac is also a stage.

I will be honored upon Pharaoh, and Egypt shall know I am the LORD. Guidance: God sometimes directs His people into a trap to spring a bigger one on their pursuers. The cul-de-sac is also a stage.

I will be honored upon Pharaoh, and Egypt shall know I am the LORD. Guidance: God sometimes directs His people into a trap to spring a bigger one on their pursuers. The cul-de-sac is also a stage.

The LORD tells Moses: turn back and camp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea. Pharaoh will say, they are tangled — the wilderness has shut them in. I will harden his heart; he will pursue. I will be honored upon Pharaoh, and Egypt shall know I am the LORD. Guidance: God sometimes directs His people into a trap to spring a bigger one on their pursuers. The cul-de-sac is also a stage.

When Pharaoh hears the people have fled, his heart and his servants' hearts turn against them: what have we done that we let Israel go from serving us? He prepares his chariot, takes his people, six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt. Guidance: Sometimes the powerful regret freeing the very people they were happy to harm. Watch how quickly relief can turn back into pursuit.

Watch how quickly relief can turn back into pursuit.

He prepares his chariot, takes his people, six hundred chosen chariots, and all the chariots of Egypt. Guidance: Sometimes the powerful regret freeing the very people they were happy to harm. Watch how quickly relief can turn back into pursuit.

The LORD hardens Pharaoh's heart; he pursues the children of Israel — who went out with a high hand. The Egyptians pursue them and overtake them encamping by the sea. Guidance: God's people may be out of Egypt but still need to be brought through. The first deliverance is not the last. Trust will be tested at the water's edge.

The LORD hardens Pharaoh's heart; he pursues the children of Israel — who went out with a high hand. The Egyptians pursue them and overtake them encamping by the sea. Guidance: God's people may be out of Egypt but still need to be brought through. The first deliverance is not the last. Trust will be tested at the water's edge.

When Pharaoh draws near, the children of Israel lift up their eyes — the Egyptians are pursuing. They are sore afraid and cry out to the LORD, then to Moses: were there no graves in Egypt that you brought us here to die? Better to have served Egypt than die in the desert. Guidance: Fear shrinks the imagination. People who saw ten plagues yesterday want to go back today. Don't be surprised when your community panics at the first cornering.

Moses: fear not. Stand still and see the salvation of the LORD, which He will show you today. For the Egyptians you have seen today, you shall see no more for ever. The LORD shall fight for you, and you shall hold your peace. Guidance: There are moments when faith looks like standing still rather than scrambling. The hardest discipline is being silent while God works.

Moses: fear not. Stand still and see the salvation of the LORD, which He will show you today. For the Egyptians you have seen today, you shall see no more for ever. The LORD shall fight for you, and you shall hold your peace. Guidance: There are moments when faith looks like standing still rather than scrambling. The hardest discipline is being silent while God works.

The LORD: why do you cry unto Me? Speak to the children of Israel that they go forward. Lift your rod, stretch your hand over the sea, divide it; the people shall go on dry ground through the midst. I will harden Egypt's heart; I will be honored upon Pharaoh and his host. Guidance: God's answer to Moses' prayer is sometimes get up. Standing still is faithful — but so is going forward at the right moment.

The angel of God moves from before Israel to behind them; the pillar of cloud moves between the two camps. It was darkness to Egypt and light to Israel; one camp did not come near the other all night. Guidance: God positions His presence as a barrier. The same cloud that lights one side is darkness on the other. Where you stand changes what God's nearness feels like.

It was darkness to Egypt and light to Israel; one camp did not come near the other all night. Guidance: God positions His presence as a barrier. The same cloud that lights one side is darkness on the other. Where you stand changes what God's nearness feels like.

Moses stretches his hand over the sea; the LORD sends a strong east wind all that night, makes the sea dry land, the waters divided. The children of Israel go into the midst of the sea on dry ground; the waters are a wall on the right and on the left. Guidance: The miracle takes all night. God doesn't always split the sea instantly — sometimes the wind has to blow until dawn before you can walk through.

Moses stretches his hand over the sea; the LORD sends a strong east wind all that night, makes the sea dry land, the waters divided. The children of Israel go into the midst of the sea on dry ground; the waters are a wall on the right and on the left. Guidance: The miracle takes all night. God doesn't always split the sea instantly — sometimes the wind has to blow until dawn before you can walk through.

The Egyptians pursue into the sea — all Pharaoh's horses, chariots, horsemen. In the morning watch the LORD looks through the pillar of fire and troubles them. He takes off their chariot wheels; they drive heavily. The Egyptians cry: let us flee — the LORD fighteth for them. Guidance: At the very moment Egypt thinks they have caught up, the wheels start coming off. Even your enemies can finally see what God has been doing.

Guidance: At the very moment Egypt thinks they have caught up, the wheels start coming off. Even your enemies can finally see what God has been doing.

The LORD: stretch your hand over the sea, that the waters may return upon the Egyptians. Moses stretches; the sea returns to its strength at morning. The Egyptians flee against it; the LORD overthrew them in the midst of the sea. The waters cover the chariots, horsemen, all the host of Pharaoh; not one of them remained. Guidance: God's mercy ends and His judgment falls in the same gesture of the same hand. The line between the two is one stretched-out arm.

The children of Israel walked on dry land in the midst of the sea, the waters a wall on the right and on the left. The LORD saved Israel; they saw the Egyptians dead on the shore. They saw the great work the LORD did; the people feared the LORD, and believed Him and His servant Moses. Guidance: Salvation always has two views — the dry path of mercy and the seashore of judgment. They end the chapter believing both the LORD and the man He sent.
